threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 243 bus is reliable—nobody who’s been here long would trust it for their morning commute.
Last night on the 59, a guy serenaded the whole bus with a saxophone. Not a single person complained. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I realized I was home when I saw a parade of dogs in tutus heading to the canal for their Saturday brunch. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, the Kottbusser Tor at 3 AM feels like a carnival of street food and laughter, a bizarre blend of midnight chaos you won't find anywhere else.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their go-to taco spot—if they mention Taqueria La Cumbre, they're probably just here for the job.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy juggling on the 14 at 16th. He dropped a ball, it rolled under the seats, and everyone just pretended it wasn’t happening.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just for Gaudí snapshots, but they miss the real charm in the small textile shops hiding in the alleys.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're new by the way they stand in the middle of Plaça del Rei, map in hand, looking utterly lost while locals just breeze by.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You keep looking for parking spots like you’re at a shopping mall. It’s a lost cause here, mate.
Last Saturday, the 333 was almost empty except for a guy loudly arguing with his own reflection in the window. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw the taxi driver get out to help a lost tourist figure out the BVG app, I knew I was home. Community doesn't just mean neighbors.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find a currywurst stand open at 3 AM, crowded with people discussing philosophy while waiting for their sausage.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they know to avoid BART delays and opt for the 33 instead, even if it means standing in line for a torta first.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 in traffic with a saxophonist playing a solo. The driver rolled the window down, and the whole bus just vibed to the street performance.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists miss how quiet Gràcia can get on a Sunday; locals are hiding away, enjoying the stillness while they recharge for the week ahead.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They're the ones marveling at the 1€ menú del dia while texting in English, oblivious to the local chatter around them.
